description_long: This object is a vintage home computer setup, consisting of three main components: a beige keyboard unit with integrated computer, a matching external disk drive, and a black joystick with a red button. The keyboard unit features a full QWERTY layout, function keys, and a series of ports and connectors on the rear side. The disk drive is rectangular with a slot for floppy disks and indicator lights, matching the computer's color scheme. The joystick is ergonomically shaped, with a prominent red button on top and a sturdy base for stability. The materials appear to be primarily plastic with some metal components for internal structure and connectors. This setup is typical of 1980s or early 1990s personal computers, often used for gaming or productivity tasks.
description: A vintage beige home computer set with a keyboard unit, external disk drive, and a black joystick with a red button. The components are made of plastic and designed for desktop use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the keyboard unit, disk drive, and joystick, with all main controls and keys visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the keyboard unit and the upright joystick, with the disk drive to the right.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the ports and connectors on the back of the keyboard unit, and the back of the joystick and disk drive.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the sloped profile of the keyboard and the joystick's ergonomic grip.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
